own_review_points: testOrderingAfterReconnects: replacing the run-check-sleep-loop with Awaitility.untilAsserted changes semantics (only need limit not exceeded repeatedly; pauseTime=100ms) -- human's main point at 2285/2291
Removing the random Thread.sleep(0-4ms) in messageListener (1222) removes the delay that originally triggered the race the test targets
Removed Thread.sleep(2000) after consumerWillBeClose.close() without sync replacement could race
Reducing receive timeouts to 200-500ms for negative assertions is generally fine
notes: V8 P1 (2148 sleep removal race) and P2 (remaining sleeps in testOrderingAfterReconnects) are reasonable/grounded = good; P7 (500ms slow consumer adequacy) = good. But P2 frames the four pauseTime sleeps as something to convert to Awaitility -- the OPPOSITE of the human's actual point (Awaitility there CHANGES the logic and should be reverted to the loop). V8 did not make the human's core 2285/2291 'Awaitility changes intention' point nor the 1222 random-delay point. P3-P6,P8,P9 are restating/praise = trivial. Missed = the 2285/2291 Awaitility-changes-logic point (human's central concern); 1222 also missed but counted as one miss-cluster. Net modest positive.
